dir.description: Displays a list of files and subdirectories in a directory.

options.desc: Displays files with specified attributes (D R H A S I L); prefix with - to negate.
Uses bare format (no heading information or summary).
Displays files in the specified directory and all subdirectories.
Lists files in sorted order (N E S D G); prefix with - to reverse.
Uses wide list format.
Pauses after each screenful of information.

copy.description: Copies one or more files to another location.

options.desc: Suppresses prompting to confirm overwriting an existing destination file.
Verifies that new files are written correctly.
Indicates an ASCII text file.
Indicates a binary file.
Prompts for confirmation before deleting each file.
Forces deletion of read-only files.
Deletes specified files from the current directory and all subdirectories.
Specifies quiet mode (do not prompt on global wildcard delete).

type.description: Displays the contents of a text file.

echo.description: Displays messages, or turns command echoing on or off.

cd.description: Displays the name of or changes the current directory.

options.desc: Changes the current drive in addition to the current directory.
Removes the directory tree, including all files and subdirectories.
Quiet mode; does not ask for confirmation when removing a directory tree.

set.description: Displays, sets, or removes environment variables.

path.description: Displays or sets a search path for executable files.

options.desc: Processes matching files in the current folder and all subfolders.
Processes folders as well.
Works on the attributes of the symbolic link versus the target of the symbolic link.
Copies directories and subdirectories except empty ones.
Copies directories and subdirectories, including empty ones.
If destination does not exist and copying more than one file, assumes destination is a directory.
Suppresses prompting to confirm overwriting an existing destination file.
Copies files changed on or after the specified date (m-d-y).

find.description: Searches for a text string in a file or files.

options.desc: Displays all lines not containing the specified string.
Counts the number of lines containing the string.
Displays line numbers with the displayed lines.
Ignores the case of characters when searching for the string.

more.description: Displays output one screen at a time.

options.desc: Clears the screen before displaying the page.
Squeezes multiple blank lines into a single line.

sort.description: Sorts input and writes the results to the screen, a file, or another device.

options.desc: Reverses the sort order (Z to A, then 9 to 0).
Sorts the file according to characters in column n.

cl.description: Microsoft C/C++ compiler driver: compiles and links C and C++ source files.

options.desc: Suppresses the compiler copyright banner.
Compiles without linking.
Specifies that all source files are C source, regardless of extension.
Specifies that all source files are C++ source, regardless of extension.
Names the executable file (glued form: /Fename).
Names the output object file (glued form: /Fofile).
Adds a directory to the list searched for include files.
Defines a preprocessor macro.
Creates fast code (maximize speed).
Sets the warning level (0-4); glued form /W4.

link.description: Microsoft incremental linker: links object files and libraries into an executable or DLL.

options.desc: Suppresses the linker copyright banner.
Overrides the default name of the program the linker creates (glued: /OUT:file).
Builds a DLL as the main output file.
Tells the operating system how to run the .exe (glued: /SUBSYSTEM:CONSOLE).
Creates debugging information for the .exe or DLL.
Overrides the environment library path (glued: /LIBPATH:dir).

lib.description: Microsoft Library Manager: creates and manages a library of object files.

options.desc: Suppresses the librarian copyright banner.
Overrides the default output file name (glued: /OUT:file).
Displays information about the output library to standard output.
Omits the specified object from the output library (glued: /REMOVE:member).

nmake.description: Microsoft Program Maintenance Utility: builds projects based on commands in a description file.

options.desc: Specifies the name of the description (makefile) to use.
Forces a build of all evaluated targets.
Displays but does not execute the commands.
Suppresses the NMAKE copyright banner.

ml.description: Microsoft Macro Assembler: assembles and links one or more assembly-language source files.

options.desc: Suppresses the assembler copyright banner.
Assembles only; does not link.
Names the output object file (glued: /Fofile).
Adds a directory to the include-file search path.
Defines a preprocessor macro.
Generates common object file format (COFF) output.
Generates CodeView debugging information.

wcc386.description: Open Watcom C/C++ 32-bit compiler: compiles C/C++ source to OMF object files.

options.desc: Operates quietly (suppresses the copyright banner).
Compiles only; does not invoke the linker.
Names the output object file (glued: -fo=file).
Adds a directory to the include-file search path (glued: -i=dir).
Defines a preprocessor macro (glued: -dNAME or -dNAME=value).
Sets the warning level (glued: -w3).
Sets the build target OS; Win32 requires -bt=nt (glued).
Uses the flat 32-bit memory model (required for Win32).
Optimizes for time (speed).
Enables the standard set of speed optimizations.

wlink.description: Open Watcom linker: links OMF object files and libraries using a directive language (NAME, FILE, LIBRARY, SYSTEM, OPTION). Directives are bare words, not slash/dash flags.
examples: wlink system nt name app.exe file hello.obj
wlink option quiet system nt name app.exe file a.obj,b.obj library kernel32.lib

wlib.description: Open Watcom library manager: creates and manages OMF object libraries. Object operands carry a +/-/* prefix.

options.desc: Operates quietly (suppresses the banner).
Names the output library file (glued: -o=file).
Suppresses creation of a backup library.

wasm.description: Open Watcom assembler: assembles x86 assembly source to OMF object files.

options.desc: Operates quietly (suppresses the copyright banner).
Names the output object file (glued: -fo=file).
Adds a directory to the include-file search path (glued: -i=dir).
Defines a preprocessor macro (glued: -dNAME).

rc.description: Microsoft Resource Compiler: compiles a resource-definition (.rc) file into a binary resource (.res) file.

options.desc: Names the output .res file.
Defines a symbol for the preprocessor.
Adds a directory to the include-file search path.
Displays messages giving the progress of the compiler (verbose).

mt.description: Microsoft Manifest Tool: generates signed files and catalogs and manages application manifests.

options.desc: Specifies the name of a manifest file to process.
Specifies the name of the output manifest (glued: /out:file).
Suppresses the startup banner.

mc.description: Microsoft Message Compiler: compiles a message-text file into resources used by an application.

options.desc: Specifies the directory for the generated header file.
Specifies the directory for the generated .rc and .bin files.
Gives verbose output.

gcc.description: GNU C compiler driver: compiles, assembles, and links C source files.

options.desc: Compiles or assembles the source files but does not link.
Places output in the named file.
Optimizes; turns on nearly all supported optimizations.
Enables all the commonly used warning messages.
Adds a directory to the search path for include files.
Predefines a preprocessor macro.

make.description: GNU make: determines which pieces of a program need to be recompiled and issues the commands to recompile them.

options.desc: Uses the named file as a makefile.
Specifies the number of jobs (commands) to run simultaneously.
Prints the commands that would be executed but does not execute them.
Continues as much as possible after an error.

mem.description: Displays the amount of used and free memory in your system.

options.desc: Classifies programs by memory usage; lists program sizes.
Displays information about the amount of free memory.
Pauses after each screenful of output.

chkdsk.description: Checks a disk and displays a status report.

options.desc: Fixes errors on the disk.
Displays the full path and name of every file on the disk.
Locates bad sectors and recovers readable information (implies /F).
